title: BUG: crashes found by fuzzing with fusil
author: devdanzin
body:
These are some crashes found by fuzzing Numpy with [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil), condensed into a single issue as requested.

### 1- `strings._expandtabs_length.reduce(numpy.zeros(200))`

```python
import numpy._core._strings

numpy._core.strings._expandtabs_length.reduce(numpy.zeros(200))
```

### Context for the issue:

I have been fuzzing Numpy using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner. I realize these crashes are unlikely to be triggered in normal usage and therefore might be of low priority.

The fuzzing was done with an ASAN free-threading clang build and not confirmed on a GILfull non-sanitizer GCC build yet.

comments.author: ngoldbaum
comments.body:
@devdanzin could you also consolidate the other unfixed open issues found by fusil into this issue?

I added the "sprintable - C" label since most of these will likely be easy to fix for someone with little familiarity with the codebase but some familiarity with native Python extensions.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
> could you also consolidate the other unfixed open issues found by fusil into this issue?

Done, except for https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/28784 because I don't know what's going on there. If it should be also consolidated and closed, let me know.

> I added the "sprintable - C" label

Thank you, that's great!

comments.author: seberg
comments.body:
After the first (which I am not sure if it is internal or not). I think the ones about no exception being set are the most user-facing here.  Others are often abuse of internal (or even testing API) and some of that is probably unsafe even if not abused.
The `setflags` is a bit interesting, user-facing, but low-level and probably should be avoided anyway.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: The segfault from calling __class__ on functions like atleast_1d was fixed in Cython: https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/7263.
